project_type: Electron desktop app (React+TS frontend, Python backend) being deployed headless on server
language: Python 3.11+ (backend), TypeScript/React (frontend - not needed for server deploy)
build_system: pip/pyproject.toml for Python, npm for Node (only Python needed for server)
test_framework: pytest with pytest-asyncio for Python, vitest for TypeScript
verification_instructions: 1. Run bash -n deploy.sh to verify syntax. 2. Check deploy.sh is executable. 3. Verify run_server.py can be imported without errors.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - full network access. Target: Ubuntu server 2GB RAM with Python 3.11+ and systemd.
contribution_requirements: Script must handle: system deps install, Python 3.11+ via deadsnakes PPA, pip deps from pyproject.toml, interactive credential prompting, config file creation, systemd service setup, error handling with colored output
key_patterns: Config stored in ~/.teleexport/config/settings.json with api_id and api_hash fields. Sessions in ~/.teleexport/sessions/. Python backend entry point is python/main.py using JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout. For headless server, need a standalone runner that bypasses IPC and runs directly.
directory_structure: teleexport/ - project root; teleexport/python/ - Python backend (core/, export/, formatters/, ipc/, utils/); teleexport/electron/ - Electron main process; teleexport/src/ - React frontend; Config: ~/.teleexport/ (sessions/, config/); Exports: ~/TeleExport/exports/
